name: Razorpay Payment Audit Trail — Structured Logging
scenario: Razorpay's logs are unstructured text, making debugging and compliance auditing a nightmare. Add JSON structured logging with MDC context so every log line is machine-parseable and includes requestId, userId, merchantId, and traceId.

core.description: pom.xml: logstash-logback-encoder. logback-spring.xml: JSON appender for prod profile, text for dev.
LoggingContextFilter: sets requestId (UUID), userId, merchantId from headers. MDC.clear() in finally.
PaymentAuditLogger: INFO log on success, ERROR on failure. Both include paymentId, gateway, amount, status as structured fields.
@SpringBootTest (3 tests): success logs INFO, failure logs ERROR, log message contains paymentId. All 3 pass.
README: sample JSON log output + 3 example Loki queries.

bonus.description: AsyncMDCService: CompletableFuture that copies and restores MDC. Test: async thread logs show same requestId.
SecureLoggingDemo: show masked card number, masked API key, no password. Code review with before/after.
LokiAlertConfig: document 3 Loki alert rules with their LogQL queries and when they fire.

ai_policy_explanation: 5 hints. MDC.clear() in finally block: mandatory — without it, thread pool reuse leaks context to next request. LogstashEncoder: outputs all MDC fields as JSON keys automatically (no extra configuration per field). ListAppender for testing: add before test, detach and clear after each test. Parameterized logging: log.info('User {}', id) NOT log.info('User ' + id) — lazy evaluation prevents string concatenation overhead when log level is disabled. Never log card numbers, CVV, API keys, or passwords.
